Fix lazy import of jq to avoid ModuleNotFoundError at import time

The 'Remove deps on jq' commit (3e801df) removed jq from ivpm.yaml/pyproject.toml
but left a top-level 'import jq' in eval_jq.py, which caused ModuleNotFoundError
when the module was imported in environments without jq installed.

Move the import inside the function so it only fails when jq is actually used.

commit.message:
Fix flaky replication-multiinstance test with retry-based state comparison (#8031)

* Initial plan

* Fix flaky replication-multiinstance test by using retry-based state comparison

Replace ensureEqualState with awaitCollectionsHaveEqualState which retries
the comparison with exponential backoff until state converges or times out.
This handles the inherent timing issues in multi-instance replication where
documents may not have fully propagated across instances when the comparison
runs.

commit.message:
fix: add kube-system DaemonSet readiness check before GitOps installation (#2972)

* Initial plan

* fix: add kube-system DaemonSet readiness check before GitOps installation

Add WaitForNamespaceDaemonSetsReady to verify all CNI DaemonSets
(e.g. Cilium) have fully re-converged after infrastructure component
installations. This prevents Flux Operator from entering
CrashLoopBackOff due to incomplete pod-to-service routing when
Cilium's BPF datapath hasn't finished programming.

Also increase apiServerStabilitySuccesses from 3 to 5 for more
robust convergence verification.

Fixes #2994

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip DaemonSets with zero desired pods in namespace readiness check

DaemonSets with DesiredNumberScheduled == 0 (e.g., due to node
selectors that don't match any nodes) should be skipped rather than
blocking readiness.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address PR review feedback for cluster stability check

- Rename waitForAPIServerStability → waitForClusterStability to reflect
  its broader scope (API server + DaemonSet readiness)
- Replace hard-coded 10.96.0.1:443 with kubernetes.default.svc:443 in
  comments
- Only swallow transient API errors (timeout/429) in
  WaitForNamespaceDaemonSetsReady; surface non-transient errors
  immediately
- Track and report blocking DaemonSet name/status on timeout for
  actionable error messages
- Extract helpers to fix cyclop/varnamelen/golines lint issues
- Add test for non-transient API error propagation
- Update architecture docs reference

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: improve variable naming and doc comments in daemonset readiness

commit.message:
Add missing GDDR telemetry (#2215)

### Issue
#2164 

### Description
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for GDDR telemetry.
It adds new data structures, APIs, and exposes telemetry tags for
capturing detailed per-module and aggregated GDDR health and error
information. The changes also update relevant enums, APIs, and bindings
to use GDDR terminology and structures throughout the codebase.

### List of the changes
**GDDR Telemetry Support:**

* Introduced a new `gddr_telemetry.hpp` header defining `BlackholeGddr`,
`GddrModuleTelemetry`, and `GddrTelemetry` structures for detailed
per-module and aggregated GDDR monitoring.
* Added functions to `FirmwareInfoProvider` and its Wormhole
implementation for retrieving per-module and aggregated GDDR telemetry,
GDDR speed, and maximum temperature, using new telemetry tags.
* Returning nullopt for SMBUS telemetry as the old and new telemetry
don't match well (and will not be needed).

**Telemetry Tag and API Updates:**

* Replaced all references to DDR with GDDR in telemetry tags and related
enums, including new tags for per-module temperatures and error counts.
* Updated all code, including firmware info providers and telemetry
readers, to use GDDR tags and structures for reading and interpreting
telemetry data.

**Python Bindings:**

* Updated nanobind Python bindings to expose new GDDR telemetry tags and
structures, and to support access to GDDR telemetry data from Python.

**Internal Refactoring and Maintenance:**

* Added necessary includes and updated CMake and source files to ensure
new telemetry structures and tags are available throughout the device
codebase.


### Testing
Manual and CI

### API Changes
/

---------

fix(scripts): post-increment exits script under set -e

bash `((pass++))` with pass=0 evaluates to 0, which returns
exit code 1 — causing immediate exit under `set -euo pipefail`.
Switch to pre-increment `((++pass))` which always evaluates to
a non-zero value.

Fixes script stopping after first check on Raspberry Pi.

commit.message:
Daily Code Quality - Perf: pre-compile image extractor regex (−7500 ns/op, −93 allocs/call) (#2961)

* perf(image): pre-compile imagePattern regex at package level

regexp.MustCompile was called inside extractImagesFromReader on every
invocation of ExtractImagesFromManifest, incurring ~7500 ns + 93 allocs
per call. Hoisting the compiled regexp to a package-level var eliminates
this overhead entirely.

Before: ~7500 ns/op, 11184 B/op, 93 allocs/op (regex compile)
After: ~3 ns/op, 0 B/op, 0 allocs/op (pre-compiled lookup)

Also adds extractor_bench_test.go covering Small/Medium/Large manifests,
multi-manifest extraction, and NormalizeImageRef variants to track
regressions as future changes are made.

fix(ui): reload config form after MA login to preserve new token

After successful MA login, the backend saves the new token directly to
config.json. But the config form still had the OLD token in its hidden
field, so 'Save and Restart' would overwrite the new token with the old.

Now calls loadConfig() after login to refresh the form with the
backend-saved values before marking dirty.

commit.message:
fix(auth): always try direct HTTP fallback on library login failure

The music_assistant_client library raises 'Invalid username or password'
for any 401 response, including format mismatches with MA beta. The
previous code short-circuited the direct HTTP fallback when it saw
'invalid' in the error message, preventing login from ever succeeding
with MA beta 2.8+.

Now always falls through to _ma_http_login() which correctly tries
both body formats and reports the actual error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(auth): support MA beta 2.8+ login format

MA beta 2.8.0b19 changed /auth/login to expect credentials in a
nested dict: {"credentials": {"username": ..., "password": ...}}
instead of flat {"username": ..., "password": ...}.

Add _ma_http_login() fallback that tries both formats when the
music_assistant_client library fails. Also update _get_ma_oauth_params()
to try both stable GET and beta JSONRPC endpoints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
